summary: The schema 0.2 reconciliation repair limits post-merge synchronization to the selected catalog Story route and preserves the guarded one-command, human-decision, explicit merge, and critical-waiver boundaries required by OCR-S-1 through OCR-S-9.
inspection_summary: At a9109350819af99df22448d6ed8bd75adf611e36, inspected the Story, architecture, generated planning-spec review request, implementation diff, affected execution-state call paths, focused unit and Story-level E2E coverage, and current persisted PR/evidence summaries. The changed source is confined to managed-worktree artifact ownership routing; the CLI, guarded-run session, human-decision checkpoint, safe-action orchestrator, and execute-merge owners are unchanged.
inspection_evidence: node --test test/execution-state.test.js test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts (56/56 passing, 2026-07-24); .vibepro/pr/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure/pr-prepare.json (current HEAD, separately not ready because strict gate reviews are stale); codebase-memory-mcp inbound traces for writeExecutionStateWithLinkedCopies and resolveArtifactRoute.

judgment_delta: Initial concern: changing managed-worktree reconciliation could expand the guarded one-command path or bypass human decision, merge, or waiver controls -> Final conclusion: the diff only replaces an undeclared probe Story with the selected PR artifact route; the control owners are unchanged, and the existing guarded-path E2E verifies the explicit authority boundary.

Initial concern: removing the probe might silently copy an unrelated profile or Story -> Final conclusion: story-scoped directory synchronization now requires both managed and source selected templates to contain the selected story_id or feature_slug route variable; the schema 0.2 catalog-and-mirror tests exercise a feature_slug profile and reconcile only the selected Story artifacts.

Initial concern: a role pass could be misread as PR or merge authorization -> Final conclusion: this is only the planning_spec product-requirement verdict; the current persisted pr-prepare remains fail-closed with stale strict gate reviews and must be regenerated after those separate gates are completed.
